title: Nova Locutio Claim Expression
description: Structured AST for claims made by the `assert` speech act. The v0.1 message schema's `assert.claim` is a string in surface syntax; this schema defines the structured form that v0.2+ of the message schema will require. Three kinds in v0.1: `predicate` (wraps an arbitrary predicate-expression), `satisfies` (subject satisfies a named property — the common case, sugar for a `predicate` with the equivalent `app('satisfies', subject, property)` shape), and `verified` (subject has been verified by some agent, optionally with a proof certificate). The predicate sub-tree is opaque at this layer; it must validate independently against predicate-expression.schema.json. Higher-level reasoning over claims (e.g. consequence-of, contradicts-with, supports) is the verifier's job and not part of this schema.

expr.description: A predicate-expression AST. Must validate against predicate-expression.schema.json; checked structurally only here.

predicate.description: An arbitrary predicate. Use when no more specific kind fits.

subject.description: Content-address of the artifact the claim is about (typically a function record).

property.description: Name of the property the subject satisfies. Resolved against the subject's own `properties[]` list (for function records).

evidence.description: Optional content-address of a proof certificate.

satisfies.description: Most common assert shape: 'subject satisfies its declared property `name`.' Sugar over a `predicate` claim of `app('satisfies', subject, property)`.

subject.description: Content-address of the artifact that has been verified.

by.description: DID of the agent that performed the verification.

proof.description: Optional content-address of the proof certificate produced.

verified.description: A meta-claim: 'this artifact has been verified by this agent (optionally with proof X).' Lets agents build attestation chains where one verifier vouches for the work of another.
